2025-26 Toronto Raptors Roster and Stats

Previous Season

Record: 46-36, Finished 5th in NBA Eastern Conference

Coach: Darko Rajakovic (46-36)

Executive: Bobby Webster

PTS/G: 114.6 (21st of 30) Opp PTS/G: 111.8 (9th of 30)

SRS: 2.75 (11th of 30) Pace: 98.4 (20th of 30)

Off Rtg: 115.9 (15th of 30) Def Rtg: 113.0 (5th of 30) Net Rtg: +2.9 (11th of 30)

Expected W-L: 48-34 (11th of 30)

Preseason Odds: Championship +10000, Over-Under 39.5

Arena: Scotiabank Arena Attendance: 772,141 (11th of 30)

NBA 2026 Playoffs:
Lost NBA Eastern Conference First Round (3-4) versus Cleveland Cavaliers  (Series Stats)

This statistic adjusts for the fact that a 3-point field goal is worth one more point than a 2-point field goal.

A measure of shooting efficiency that takes into account 2-point field goals, 3-point field goals, and free throws.

Win Shares Per 48 Minutes

Box Plus/Minus

Box Plus/Minus
A box score estimate of the points per 100 possessions a player contributed above a league-average player, translated to an average team.

Scottie Barnes 4.2 (17th)

Defensive Box Plus/Minus

Defensive Box Plus/Minus
A box score estimate of the defensive points per 100 possessions a player contributed above a league-average player, translated to an average team.

Scottie Barnes 2.3 (6th)

Value Over Replacement Player

Value over Replacement Player
A box score estimate of the points per 100 TEAM possessions that a player contributed above a replacement-level (-2.0) player, translated to an average team and prorated to an 82-game season.

Multiply by 2.70 to convert to wins over replacement.

Scottie Barnes 4.2 (10th)

Offensive Rating

Offensive Rating
An estimate of points produced (players) or scored (teams) per 100 possessions

Assist Pct

Assist Percentage
An estimate of the percentage of teammate field goals a player assisted while they were on the floor.

Jamal Shead 30.6 (19th)

Block Pct

Block Percentage
An estimate of the percentage of opponent two-point field goal attempts blocked by the player while they were on the floor.

Scottie Barnes 4.2 (12th)

Triple-Doubles

Triple-Doubles The NBA did not track Triple-Doubles until 1979-80 when Magic Johnson popularized the feat. Nonetheless, we can present Triple-Doubles since 1950-51 when rebounds became an official statistic. A small number may yet be unaccounted for but we're confident in the completeness of the list.
